name: Dark energy equation-of-state evolution (DESI hint)
summary: DESI Year 1 BAO data combined with Type Ia supernovae and Planck CMB shows a ~2-4 sigma preference for time-evolving dark energy (w_0 > -1, w_a < 0 in the Chevallier-Polarski-Linder parameterisation) over the cosmological-constant null hypothesis w = -1. If real, dark energy is not a true constant Lambda but a dynamical scalar field whose effect grows then decreases - a striking deviation from LambdaCDM. Significance is sample-dependent (Pantheon+, Union3, DES-SN5YR give different preferences) and not yet confirmed.

characteristic_numbers.note: Most-significant SN sample combination (Union3 + DESI Y1 BAO + CMB).
Time-evolution parameter. Preference for w_a < 0 is the new feature.
Sample-dependent: Pantheon+ ~2.5 sigma, Union3 ~3.5 sigma, DES-SN5YR ~3.9 sigma.

principles_stressed.note: If confirmed, ends the LambdaCDM era and replaces the cosmological-constant problem with a dynamical-DE problem. Quintessence, k-essence, DGP, holographic DE all become directly testable. The DESI hint will be tested by DESI-Y3 (2026), DES-Y6, Euclid, LSST/Rubin.

history: DESI Collaboration (2024), 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological constraints from BAO measurements', JCAP 02, 021 (2025), arXiv:2404.03002. DESI 2024 IV (DESI Collaboration, arXiv:2404.03000) is the BAO measurement paper. SNe systematic-induced variation explored in Notari-Redi-Tesi (2024), arXiv:2406.08459 and many follow-ups. Active controversy; will be resolved by DESI-Y3.

references.citation: DESI Collaboration, 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological constraints from BAO measurements', JCAP 02, 021 (2025)
